messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following insurance application privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"InsureCo processes your personal and health data to assess your insurance application and calculate premiums. We rely on your explicit consent for processing health information. Decisions may involve automated profiling based on your age, health history, and lifestyle. You have the right to request human review of any automated decision. Data is shared with reinsurers and fraud prevention networks. Records are retained for the duration of your policy plus 7 years. Contact dataprotection@insureco.com for queries. Our DPO is at dpo@insureco.com. We notify the relevant authority within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Consent stated for health data. Insurance contract implies Art. 6(1)(b) for personal data.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Health data correctly identified. Explicit consent obtained per Art. 9(2)(a).

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Application assessment, premium calculation, reinsurance, and fraud prevention are all stated as purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Age, health history, and lifestyle profiling may be broad. No explicit statement that only necessary data is collected for each purpose.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Policy duration plus 7 years is clearly stated and proportionate.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Right to human review of automated decisions (Art. 22) is stated. Other rights not individually listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Reinsurers and fraud prevention networks mentioned as categories. Individual identities and DPA references not provided.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. Reinsurers and fraud networks often operate globally.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact disclosed at dpo@insureco.com.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour authority notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 15-21 (most rights not listed), Art. 28 (processors not identified), Art. 46 (international transfer safeguards absent)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Add full data subject rights list with contact instructions for each
- Name specific reinsurers and fraud network processors or link to a processor list
- Address international transfer safeguards for any non-EEA reinsurers or fraud networks
